items.title: Event propagation and delegation: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? preventDefault also stops the event from reaching ancestor listeners.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. preventDefault cancels a cancelable default action; propagation controls are separate.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. preventDefault cancels a cancelable default action; propagation controls are separate.

items.title: Event propagation and delegation: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Handle capture, target and bubble phases and delegate stable ancestor events when appropriate. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating delegated bubbling event from stable ancestor; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: // TODO: implement the declared bounded browser behavior.

answer.referenceSolution: const list = document.querySelector("ul"); list.addEventListener("click", (event) => { const button = event.target.closest("button"); if (button) document.querySelector("output").textContent = button.dataset.value; }); const form = document.querySelector("form"); form.addEventListener("submit", (event) => { event.preventDefault(); const input = form.elements.namedItem("topic"); document.querySelector("output").textContent = input.value.trim() || "required"; });
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded javascript artifact for delegated bubbling event from stable ancestor. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
